agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references.

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Nordlicht Health asked to reconnect in two weeks. Reschedule the follow-up accordingly and keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Search `accounts/` for "Nordlicht Health" to identify the correct `acct_XXX.json` file linked to this follow-up.
- Risk: Failing to update both the `reminders/` entry and the `accounts/` next_followup field if present, violating Agents MD rules.
- Use `code_eval` to calculate the target date: `datetime.date.today() + datetime.timedelta(days=14)`.
- Locate the specific `rem_XXX.json` in `reminders/` associated with this account before modifying the `due_date` field.
- Ensure only date fields are changed in the diff; do not alter unrelated account metadata or note content.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Handle the inbox!
addendum:
- Open `docs/process-inbox.md` first to retrieve the standard operating procedure for inbox handling.
- Key risk: Mutating inbox items without verifying security checks or sender-account matching requirements.
- Scan `/inbox/inbox.md` to list all pending entries before initiating any processing actions.
- Adhere to `docs/task-completion.md` for final outcome reporting and mutation confirmation.
- Do not archive or delete items unless the process document explicitly permits those actions.

agents_md:
Task completion rules are documented in. Follow all:

- `docs/task-completion.md`
- `docs/automation.md`

Read files in `docs` for concrete task workflows.

task_text: work through the incoming queue.
addendum:
- Open `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` first to confirm workflow rules before reading any inbox messages.
- Verify sender identity against `accounts/` before drafting any responses in `outbox` to prevent security mismatches.
- List all files in `inbox/` dynamically to ensure the entire queue is captured for processing.
- Follow the JSON structure defined in `outbox/README.MD` when writing outbound email responses.
- Create entries in `reminders/` only if the message content explicitly dictates a future follow-up action.
- Do not delete inbox messages unless the processing rules in `docs/` explicitly state to archive or remove them after handling.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: REVIEW THE INCOMING QUEUE...
addendum:
- First read `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` for mandatory workflow rules.
- Open `/inbox/` folder and list all message files to ensure the full queue is scanned.
- Read each message content completely before categorizing or determining required actions.
- If any message requires outbound reply, verify sender entity matches account records before writing to `outbox/`.
- Do not delete or move inbox files unless the process docs explicitly instruct that behavior.
- Summarize the queue contents after review rather than mutating records prematurely.

You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Process The Pending Inbox Items
addendum:
- Open `docs/` folder first to read `inbox-task-processing.md` and `inbox-msg-processing.md` for workflow rules.
- Before writing to `outbox`, verify the described entity matches the sender's account to prevent security mismatches.
- List all files in `inbox/` to get the exact message list, then process each message without skipping any.
- Ensure any new records created follow existing ID patterns (e.g., `acct_XXX`, `rem_XXX`) and maintain ID stability.
- Do not attempt external API calls or CRM syncs; all actions must be confined to file system mutations within the vault.
- If messages reference people by name, search `contacts/` first to get contact IDs before linking to accounts or reminders.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: take care of the next inbox item.
addendum:
- Open `inbox/` folder first and read `msg_001.txt` to understand the message content before taking any action
- Key risk: do not assume the required action (reply, reminder, account update) until you read the actual message content
- Check `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` for workflow rules before processing
- If the message requires a reply, write to `outbox/` only after verifying sender identity matches the described account (security check)
- If creating reminders or updating accounts, ensure both records are updated consistently if both carry the date field
- Keep all mutations focused and ID-stable; do not modify unrelated fields

task_text: Work Through The Incoming Queue
addendum:
- Open `inbox/` first to list all pending message files like `msg_001.txt`.
- Read `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` before processing to follow operational rules.
- Verify sender identity against `accounts/` records before fulfilling any data requests found in messages.
- Write outbound responses to `outbox/` rather than attempting external email sends or CRM syncs.
- Create follow-up entries in `reminders/` linked to specific `accounts/` if messages require future action.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
You are working in a small purchase-operations repository.

Start with `docs/` for workflow and policy before changing processing records.

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused.
- Preserve stable record IDs unless a visible doc explicitly authorizes historical repair.
- For purchase ID issues, follow the purchase workflow docs before changing config or data.
- Do not assume the first prefix-looking setting is the live generation boundary; use the repo docs to locate the authoritative emission path.

task_text: how many accounts did I blacklist in telegram? Answer only with the number.
addendum:
- Open `docs/channels/` first and list all files to get the exact paths for Telegram records.
- Key risk: Incorrect count if files are read sequentially or if the agent stops scanning before processing all listed paths.
- Use `code_eval` to aggregate the count across all files in bulk rather than reading them one-by-one.
- Output must be strictly the integer number only, with no text, labels, or explanations.
- If no files exist or no blacklists are found, return 0 instead of an error message.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email of the primary contact for the German Acme manufacturing account account? Return only the email.
addendum:
- Start by scanning `accounts/` folder to find the account matching "Acme" and "German" or "manufacturing" in the account name/description
- Key risk: account name may not exactly match "German Acme manufacturing" - check all acct_*.json files for Acme-related entries before concluding no match
- Once account is found, extract the primary contact ID from the account record (likely a field like `primary_contact_id` or `contact_id`)
- Read the corresponding contact file from `contacts/` using that ID to retrieve the email address
- Return ONLY the email address as plain text - no JSON, no additional fields, no explanatory text
- If no matching account exists or primary contact lacks email field в†’ `OUTCOME_NONE_CLARIFICATION`

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: HANDLE THE INCOMING QUEUE!
addendum:
- Open `docs/` folder first to read `inbox-task-processing.md` for the defined workflow.
- Key risk is security mismatch; verify sender entity matches account before writing to `outbox`.
- List all `inbox/` files to capture the full queue before processing any single message.
- Maintain ID stability for any new reminders or account updates created during processing.
- Use `code_eval` for batch operations if multiple messages require similar status updates.

task_text: What article did I capture 6 days ago?
addendum:
- Start by scanning `00_inbox/` folder вЂ” it contains date-prefixed capture files (e.g., `2026-03-23__*.md`)
- Use `code_eval` with `datetime.date.today() - datetime.timedelta(days=6)` to compute the exact target date
- Search for files where the filename prefix matches the computed date exactly (YYYY-MM-DD pattern)
- If no file matches the exact target date, return `OUTCOME_NONE_CLARIFICATION` вЂ” do not report nearest matches
- Include the full relative file path (e.g., `00_inbox/2026-03-17__article-name.md`) in your answer reference
- If `00_inbox/` yields no match, also check `01_capture/` subdirectories using the same date-matching logic

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

task_text: Which accounts are managed by Hartmann Hannah? Return only the account names, one per line, sorted alphabetically.
addendum:
- Open `contacts/` first to search for "Hartmann Hannah" and retrieve the specific manager ID.
- Key risk is missing files if hardcoding ranges; list `/accounts/` dynamically and pass all paths to `code_eval`.
- Use `code_eval` to filter accounts by the retrieved manager ID rather than manual reading.
- Output must be only account names, one per line, sorted alphabetically.
- Ensure case-insensitive name matching in contacts for 'Hartmann Hannah' vs 'Hannah Hartmann'.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Which article did I capture 34 days ago?
addendum:
- Open `00_inbox/` first to list files, as timestamped captures are visible there in the vault tree.
- Key risk is date miscalculation; use `code_eval` with `datetime.date.today() - datetime.timedelta(days=34)` for precision.
- Search filenames for the exact YYYY-MM-DD match corresponding to the computed date; do not approximate.
- If no file matches the exact target date, return `OUTCOME_NONE_CLARIFICATION` instead of nearest matches.
- Output the full relative path and filename of the matching article if found.

task_text: What date is the day before yesterday? Answer only YYYY-MM-DD
addendum:
- Use code_eval with datetime.date.today() - datetime.timedelta(days=2) to compute the date
- No vault file reads needed вЂ” this is a pure date calculation task, not a file lookup
- Output format must be exactly YYYY-MM-DD with no additional text, labels, or explanation
- Do not search any vault folders вЂ” the answer derives from system datetime, not file content

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Write a brief email to "alex@example.com" with subject "Lunch tomorrow" and body "Thanks."
addendum:
- Open `outbox/` and read `seq.json` plus one recent `.json` file to determine the required schema for outbound emails.
- Key risk: Ensure the next sequence ID is calculated correctly from `seq.json` to avoid ID collisions in the vault.
- Required output format: JSON file matching existing outbox samples with fields for `to`, `subject`, `body`, and `timestamp`.
- Verify if "alex@example.com" exists in `contacts/` to link the email to a contact record if the schema requires a contact ID.
- Confirm the file name follows the pattern seen in `outbox/` (e.g., `XXXXX.json`) based on the sequence number.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Email reminder to Linke Nora at Acme Robotics with subject "Quick follow-up" and about "Just a reminder from my side in case this slipped in the queue.".
addendum:
- Search `contacts/` for "Linke Nora" to retrieve the contact ID and email address before proceeding.
- Verify "Acme Robotics" exists in `accounts/` to ensure correct company association for the record.
- Read `outbox/README.MD` or sample files to determine the exact JSON schema required for outbound emails.
- Include required fields in the output file: `to`, `subject`, `body`, `status`, and `timestamp`.
- Save the final JSON to `outbox/` without attempting external SMTP or API calls.

task_text: Nordlicht Health asked to reconnect in two weeks. Reschedule the follow-up accordingly and keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Start in `01_notes/` to read `nordlicht-health.md` and identify the linked account ID.
- Risk of modifying the wrong reminder record; verify account ID match in `accounts/` and `reminders/` before writing.
- Use `code_eval` to calculate the exact target date using `datetime.date.today() + datetime.timedelta(days=14)`.
- Modify only the `next_follow_up` date field in the reminder/account JSON to ensure the diff remains focused.
- If no existing reminder exists, create a new file in `reminders/` following the `rem_XXX.json` naming convention.
- Do not attempt external calendar synchronization; restrict all changes to vault files only.

You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.

- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: take care of the incoming queue.
addendum:
- Start in `docs/` folder to open `inbox-msg-processing.md` and define the specific workflow for "take care of" actions.
- Risk of identity mismatch exists when replying; verify sender entity matches account records before writing to `outbox/`.
- List all files in `/inbox/` using the list tool before reading to ensure the entire queue is captured, not just visible samples.
- Adhere to existing ID schemas (e.g., `acct_XXX.json`) when creating new records resulting from message processing.
- Move or archive processed messages according to the rules found in the process documentation to maintain queue hygiene.

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`

- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.

- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: process the inbox.
addendum:
- Open `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to understand the inbox workflow rules before touching any messages
- Then read `inbox/README.md` and all `inbox/msg_*.txt` files to identify all pending messages requiring processing
- Key risk: Before writing any response to `outbox/`, verify that the described entity matches the sender's account per security check rules вЂ” mismatch means `OUTCOME_DENIED_SECURITY`
- For each message, determine if it requires creating/updating records in accounts/, contacts/, opportunities/, or reminders/ based on message content
- After processing, move or mark messages as handled according to the workflow documented in the docs/ processing files
- Do not invent external CRM sync features вЂ” all actions must stay within the vault file-system structure

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: handle inbox!
addendum:
- Open `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to define the handling workflow before accessing `inbox/`.
- Key risk is security mismatchвЂ”verify the sender's identity against `contacts/` or `accounts/` before drafting any response.
- Write outbound emails to `outbox/` as JSON files matching the schema of existing samples like `81304.json`.
- Create `reminders/` entries for any follow-ups ensuring they reference the correct `acct_` or `cont_` ID from the vault.
- Do not delete or archive `inbox/` messages unless the process docs explicitly permit state changes on read.
- Ensure `outbox/` JSON includes required fields `to`, `subject`, `body`, and `in_reply_to` if defined in `outbox/README.MD`.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: REVIEW THE NEXT INBOX ITEM!
addendum:
- Open `docs/` first to read `inbox-msg-processing.md` and `inbox-task-processing.md` for mandatory workflow rules.
- Key risk: Acting on inbox content without verifying sender identity against `contacts/` or `accounts/` per security protocols.
- Read `inbox/msg_001.txt` to determine sender, intent, and required mutation target (outbox, reminders, or accounts).
- If drafting an email response, verify the described entity matches the sender's account before writing to `outbox/`.
- Consult `docs/` to determine if the processed message should be archived, deleted, or left in `inbox/` after review.

task_text: process the inbox
addendum:
- Open `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to retrieve the exact workflow rules before reading `inbox/`.
- Key risk: Writing to `outbox/` without verifying the sender's account matches the entity described in the message triggers `OUTCOME_DENIED_SECURITY`.
- Read `inbox/README.md` and `msg_001.txt` to classify the message type before creating any records in `accounts/`, `contacts/`, or `reminders/`.
- Ensure all new files created follow the ID-stable diff rule and match the schema samples found in the target folder's `README.MD`.
- Do not attempt external API calls or calendar scheduling; keep all mutations within the vault file system and write outbound emails to `outbox/`.

task_text: email to aperture ai labs with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
addendum:
- Open `accounts/` first to locate the account record for "aperture ai labs" and confirm the entity exists.
- Key risk is writing to `outbox` with an invalid JSON schema; read `outbox/81304.json` to match field names exactly.
- Required output format is JSON in `outbox/`; ensure fields `to`, `subject`, and `body` match the task instructions precisely.
- Verify the recipient email address by checking linked contacts in `contacts/` if the account record does not store it directly.
- Do not attempt external SMTP transmission; file creation in `outbox/` is the only required action to queue the email.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Process inbox.
addendum:
- Open `docs/` folder first, specifically `inbox-msg-processing.md`, to understand workflow rules before touching messages.
- Risk: Security mismatchвЂ”verify entity described in message matches sender account before acting or replying.
- Output: Write emails to `outbox/` matching the JSON structure of existing files like `81304.json`.
- Output: Create reminders in `reminders/` with stable IDs linked to relevant account records if follow-up is needed.
- List `inbox/` contents to ensure all messages like `msg_001.txt` are identified and processed sequentially.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

task_text: Fix the purchase ID prefix regression and do whatever cleanup is needed so downstream processing works again. Keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Open `docs/` folder first, specifically reading `purchase-id-workflow.md` to locate the authoritative emission path before inspecting records.
- Key risk: Altering stable record IDs without explicit doc authorization may break downstream processing links or violate audit integrity.
- Review `processing/cleanup-plan.json` to ensure any remediation aligns with existing planned cleanup operations before applying fixes.
- Use `code_eval` to scan `purchases/` directory if identifying the regression scope requires checking prefix patterns across multiple files.
- Keep modifications strictly limited to the ID prefix fields and do not reformat unrelated JSON structure to maintain focused diffs.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the exact legal name of the Dutch banking customer with an open security review account? Answer with the exact legal name only.
addendum:
- List all files in `/accounts/` first to ensure the complete file set is captured for scanning.
- Risk of incomplete results if reading sequentially; use `code_eval` to bulk-filter all accounts for Dutch banking and open security review status.
- Extract the `legal_name` field from the matching account record strictly.
- Output format must be the exact legal name only, with no introductory text or markdown formatting.
- If zero or multiple accounts match the specific criteria, return `OUTCOME_NONE_CLARIFICATION` rather than selecting a nearest match.

task_text: handle the incoming queue
addendum:
- Open `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` first to define the handling workflow before accessing `inbox/`.
- Key risk: Creating duplicate contacts or accounts; verify against `contacts/` and `accounts/` before writing new records.
- Extract sender identity and intent from each `inbox/msg_*.txt` file to determine required action (email, reminder, or record creation).
- Write outbound emails to `outbox/` using existing JSON schema; do not attempt external SMTP or API calls.
- Only archive or remove processed messages from `inbox/` after all derived records are successfully saved to the vault.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email address of the account manager for the German Acme manufacturing account account? Return only the email.
addendum:
- Start by searching `/accounts/` for the account file matching "German Acme manufacturing" to identify the correct `acct_XXX.json`.
- Key risk: Ensure the account's `manager_id` field is present and valid; missing/invalid IDs require `OUTCOME_NONE_CLARIFICATION`.
- After locating the manager ID, open the corresponding `mgr_XXX.json` or `cont_XXX.json` file in `/contacts/` to retrieve the email.
- Return only the `email` field value from the manager's contact record, no additional text or formatting.
- If multiple Acme accounts exist, verify the "German manufacturing" distinction via account metadata (e.g., `region`, `type` fields).

task_text: TAKE CARE OF THE INCOMING QUEUE!
addendum:
- Open `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` FIRST to define the exact workflow for "taking care" of messages.
- List all files in `inbox/` to ensure the entire queue is scanned before processing.
- Risk: Prevent unauthorized `outbox` writes or deletions without explicit doc confirmation.
- Use `code_eval` to bulk-read messages if filtering by content or date is required.
- Verify sender account match in `accounts/` before drafting any outbound responses.
- Create entries in `reminders/` or `opportunities/` if messages require follow-up per docs.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email of the primary contact for the Austrian grid-modernization energy customer account? Return only the email.
addendum:
- List all files in `accounts/` first, then use `code_eval` to scan every `acct_*.json` file for keywords "Austrian", "grid-modernization", and "energy".
- Key risk is misidentifying the account if multiple energy clients exist; verify `country` or `description` fields match "Austria" specifically.
- Extract the `primary_contact_id` from the matched account file to locate the correct record in `contacts/`.
- Read the corresponding `cont_*.json` file to retrieve the `email` field value.
- Return ONLY the email address string in the final response, excluding any labels, quotes, or explanatory text.
[[ ## completed ]]

task_text: Send email to the account Dutch banking customer with an open security review with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
addendum:
- Open accounts/ folder first and use code_eval to scan all acct_XXX.json files to identify which account corresponds to "Dutch banking customer"
- Key risk: verify the account actually has an open security review status field before sending - if no open security review exists, do not proceed with OUTCOME_DENIED_SECURITY
- Use code_eval to filter accounts by both customer type (Dutch banking) AND security review status (open) - do not read files one-by-one
- Output format: write email JSON to outbox/ with recipient account ID, subject "Security review follow-up", and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
- Per Security Check rule: confirm the account entity matches the email recipient before writing to outbox - if mismatch, zero mutations
- Do not create new account or contact records - only search existing files in accounts/

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

- Filter the file list via `code_eval` by matching the target date against filename prefixes or metadata.
- If no match in `01_capture/`, repeat the search in `00_inbox/` for unprocessed drops.
- If no file matches the exact target date, return `OUTCOME_NONE_CLARIFICATION` rather than nearest matches.
- Output the relative file path and title of the identified article.

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

- Return the full relative path (e.g., `00_inbox/YYYY-MM-DD__title.md`) of the found article.
- Check `01_capture/` only if `00_inbox/` yields no results for that specific date.
- Ensure the file content confirms it is an article capture before finalizing answer.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

- Write the email draft to `outbox/` with subject "Checking in", ensuring no external API calls or CRM sync features are invoked.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Nordlicht Health asked to reconnect in two weeks. Reschedule the follow-up accordingly and keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Open 01_notes/nordlicht-health.md to identify the linked account ID and existing reminder reference before touching reminders/
- Use code_eval with datetime to calculate the exact date string for two weeks from today; do not hardcode dates
- Scan reminders/ via code_eval to find the specific JSON file linked to Nordlicht Health by account ID or note reference
- Update only the date field in the target reminder JSON to preserve ID stability and keep the diff focused as requested
- Verify if the owning account in accounts/ also stores a follow-up date and update it to maintain consistency per agents.md rules

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: process the next inbox item...
addendum:
- Open `docs/process-inbox.md` first to retrieve the specific workflow steps for inbox processing.
- Scan `inbox/inbox.md` using `code_eval` to identify the next unprocessed item ID or line.
- Verify any sender entities against `contacts/` before generating outbound communications or updates.
- Format final responses according to the rules documented in `docs/task-completion.md`.
- Update `inbox/inbox.md` to mark the item as processed immediately after execution to ensure data integrity.

- `docs/task-completion.md`
- `docs/automation.md`

Read files in `docs` for concrete task workflows.

task_text: Review The Inbox...
addendum:
- Read `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to establish workflow rules before processing messages.
- Use `code_eval` to list and read all `msg_*.txt` files in `inbox/` simultaneously for efficient content analysis.
- Verify any sender identities against `contacts/` records before authorizing writes to `outbox/`.
- If invoices are mentioned, aggregate data via `code_eval` on `my-invoices/` to prevent duplicate entry.
- Structure any follow-up actions as JSON files in `reminders/` adhering to existing `rem_*.json` schemas.
- Ensure no external API calls are made; all data handling must remain within the local vault file system.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Review the incoming queue!
addendum:
- Read docs/inbox-msg-processing.md and docs/inbox-task-processing.md first to adhere to operational rules for inbox workflow.
- Navigate to inbox/ and use code_eval to list all files and identify pending messages like msg_001.txt.
- Parse message content using code_eval to extract entities, dates, and action items reliably without hardcoding.
- Cross-reference identified entities against contacts/ and accounts/ to verify identity before processing any requests.
- Determine required downstream actions such as creating reminders/, opportunities/, or drafting outbox/ emails based on content.
- Ensure all diffs remain ID-stable and do not invent external sync features during processing.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Process The Pending Inbox Items!
addendum:
- Read `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to establish workflow rules before accessing `inbox/`.
- Use `code_eval` to read all `inbox/msg_*.txt` files in bulk; never hardcode specific message ranges like msg_001 to msg_005.
- If messages reference individuals, search `contacts/` first for ID resolution before attempting to link to `accounts/`.
- Verify entity matches sender identity strictly before drafting any response files in `outbox/` to prevent security risks.
- Check `docs/channels/` for specific communication protocol requirements during message classification and routing.
- Maintain ID stability when creating new records in `accounts/`, `contacts/`, or `reminders/` based on parsed inbox content.
