agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Work Through The Pending Inbox Items.
addendum:
- Read docs/inbox-msg-processing.md and docs/inbox-task-processing.md first for workflow rules.
- List inbox/ files excluding README.md to identify pending messages like msg_001.txt.
- Verify sender identity against contacts/ or 01_notes/ before writing to outbox/.
- Match existing outbox/ JSON structure (e.g., 81304.json) for outbound responses.
- Use code_eval to parse message content for entities or dates.
- Archive processed items per inbox/README.md rules without deleting original data.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Handle the inbox queue
addendum:
- Read docs/inbox-msg-processing.md and docs/inbox-task-processing.md to establish workflow rules before accessing /inbox.
- Use code_eval to list all files in /inbox and identify pending messages like msg_001.txt for processing.
- Verify sender entities against contacts/ and accounts/ before creating records or writing to /outbox.
- Draft outbound communications in /outbox following the JSON structure of existing samples like 81304.json.
- Use code_eval with datetime for any relative dates found within message content to ensure accuracy.
- Maintain ID stability by avoiding manual renaming of files during the queue handling process.

You are working in a small purchase-operations repository.

Start with `docs/` for workflow and policy before changing processing records.

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused.
- Preserve stable record IDs unless a visible doc explicitly authorizes historical repair.
- For purchase ID issues, follow the purchase workflow docs before changing config or data.
- Do not assume the first prefix-looking setting is the live generation boundary; use the repo docs to locate the authoritative emission path.

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

- Use code_eval to search all files in accounts/ and reminders/ for Blue Harbor Bank instead of assuming file IDs.
- Apply the date change 2026-03-05 to both the reminder and owning account records to maintain system consistency.
- Validate the date format strictly using datetime tools to ensure exact match requirements are met.
- Restrict changes to only the follow-up date field to keep the diff focused and maintain ID stability.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the exact legal name of the Dutch port-operations shipping account account? Answer with the exact legal name only.
addendum:
- Read `01_notes/canalport-shipping.md` to identify the specific account ID linked to the Dutch port-operations entity.
- Use `code_eval` to scan all `accounts/acct_*.json` files rather than hardcoding specific account numbers.
- Match the account ID or entity details from the note to the corresponding JSON record in `accounts/`.
- Extract the exact legal name field from the verified account JSON file.
- Output only the legal name string with no additional text, punctuation, or markdown.

task_text: Process The Next Inbox Item
addendum:
- Read docs/inbox-msg-processing.md first to establish the required workflow steps before accessing message files
- Use code_eval to list inbox/ contents and select the lowest numbered msg_*.txt file for processing
- Verify any sender identity against contacts/ records before authorizing writes to outbox/ or accounts/
- Parse message content using code_eval to extract dates or amounts rather than hardcoding values
- Ensure any new records created in reminders/ or opportunities/ match the existing JSON schema found in those folders
- Write all outbound communications as JSON files to outbox/ instead of attempting external email transmission

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: which captured article is from 43 days ago?
addendum:
- Navigate to `01_capture/` and use `code_eval` to generate a full recursive file list, avoiding hardcoded ranges.
- Calculate the exact target date (Today minus 43 days) using `datetime` within `code_eval` for relative date accuracy.
- Filter the file list against the calculated YYYY-MM-DD prefix to identify the matching captured article.
- Treat `01_capture/` contents as immutable read-only sources per `agents_md` during inspection.
- Output the relative path of the found file; if none match, report zero results without fallback to `00_inbox/`.

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

task_text: What is the email address of the account manager for the Austrian grid-modernization energy customer account? Return only the email.
addendum:
- Use code_eval to scan all accounts/*.json files for the Austrian grid-modernization energy description to identify the account ID.
- Search contacts/ using code_eval to find mgr_*.json records linked to the identified account ID.
- Prioritize contacts/ directory for all person-related data retrieval instead of embedded account fields.
- Verify the contact role field explicitly matches account manager before extraction.
- Return only the raw email address string without markdown, quotes, or additional text.
- Check 01_notes/greengrid-energy.md for context if account JSON descriptions are ambiguous.

- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.

- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

- Enforce security heuristic by verifying entity matches sender before outbox writes.
- Format output as JSON matching existing `outbox/*.json` samples (subject, body, to).
- Write final email file to `outbox/` using the generated sequence number.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email address of Krause Christian? Return only the email
addendum:
- Navigate to `contacts/` and use `code_eval` to scan all JSON files for "Krause Christian".
- Adhere to People heuristic: search `contacts/` only; do not query `accounts/` or `01_notes/` for this data.
- Verify the matched record contains a valid `email` field before extraction.
- Return strictly the email address string with no preamble, labels, or markdown formatting.
- If no exact match is found, check for name variations like "Christian Krause" within the same directory.

- Preserve existing record IDs and filenames to maintain ID-stability; only modify the date field and necessary metadata.
- Update both the reminders entry and the accounts record if both contain follow-up date fields, per operational rules.
- Ensure the final diff is focused only on the date change and timestamp updates, avoiding whitespace or unrelated field modifications.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Send email to Aperture AI Labs with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
addendum:
- Search `contacts/` using `code_eval` filtering for "Aperture" to locate the relevant person ID and linked account.
- Open `01_notes/aperture-ai-labs.md` to confirm context and verify the account ID matches the contact record.
- Use `code_eval` on `docs/channels/` and read `outbox/README.MD` to determine the exact JSON structure required for outbound emails.
- Verify entity details match the intended recipient before writing any data to the `outbox/` directory.
- Write the email JSON to `outbox/` with the specified subject and body, ensuring no hardcoded ranges are used.
- Check `reminders/` for existing follow-up tasks related to this security review to update or close them.

task_text: handle the inbox queue.
addendum:
- Open `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to establish workflow rules before processing messages.
- List all files in `inbox/` to identify pending items, then read each message to determine required action type.
- Verify sender entity matches existing records in `contacts/` BEFORE drafting any response or writing to `outbox/`.
- Use `code_eval` to parse message content for dates or amounts if converting tasks to `reminders/` or `opportunities/`.
- Ensure any new records created follow the JSON schema found in existing `contacts/` or `accounts/` samples to maintain ID stability.
- Keep all state changes within the vault file system; do not attempt external CRM sync or API calls.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: process the inbox
addendum:
- Path: Read docs/inbox-msg-processing.md and docs/inbox-task-processing.md first to establish workflow rules before accessing messages.
- Path: Open /inbox to read README.md and all msg_*.txt files to identify specific actions required for each message.
- Risk: Verify entity matches sender before writing any response drafts to the outbox folder to prevent unauthorized communication.
- Tooling: Use code_eval to list and parse all messages in /inbox dynamically rather than hardcoding file ranges like msg_001.
- Format: Ensure any new records created in reminders or accounts match the existing JSON schema found in those respective folders.
- Security: Keep all state changes within the typed file-system; do not attempt external CRM syncs or API calls during processing.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Send short follow-up email to Alex Meyer about next steps on the expansion. Keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Search contacts directory first using code_eval to locate Alex Meyer's ID and linked account record.
- Review opportunities and 01_notes folders for specific expansion project details to contextualize next steps.
- Verify entity ownership matches sender identity before authorizing any write to the outbox directory.
- Format output JSON to match existing outbox samples such as 81305.json ensuring schema compliance.
- Write email file to outbox keeping the diff focused and avoiding changes to unrelated metadata.
- Confirm no external API calls are used; rely on the file-system write to trigger send per agents.md.

task_text: HANDLE THE PENDING INBOX ITEMS.
addendum:

- Use `code_eval` to count and filter all inbox entries dynamically instead of hardcoding file ranges.
- Verify entity identity matches sender details strictly before moving processed items to `outbox/`.
- Format output logs according to `docs/task-completion.md` to ensure status changes are tracked consistently.
- Apply `datetime` via `code_eval` for any relative date filtering required during inbox triage.
- Check `docs/automation.md` to determine if specific inbox items trigger automated downstream actions.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Handle The Incoming Queue
addendum:
- Path: Read `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to establish workflow rules before accessing `inbox/` messages.
- Tooling: Use `code_eval` to list all `.txt` files in `inbox/` for queue count; NEVER hardcode message ranges like `msg_001..010`.
- Risk: Verify sender identity against `contacts/` JSON records before drafting any response in `outbox/` to ensure security compliance.
- Format: Extract actionable entities from messages and link them to existing IDs in `accounts/` or `contacts/` before creating new records.
- Path: If messages require follow-up, create entries in `reminders/` ensuring ID stability and updating owning account dates if applicable.
- Tooling: Use `code_eval` with `datetime` for any relative dates found in messages to schedule reminders accurately.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: TAKE CARE OF THE PENDING INBOX ITEMS.
addendum:
- Read `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to establish workflow rules before touching `inbox/`.
- Scan `inbox/` for pending files (e.g., `msg_001.txt`) and use `code_eval` to parse content for entities and dates.
- Verify sender identity against `contacts/` or `accounts/` before drafting any responses in `outbox/` to ensure security.
- Create new records in `opportunities/` or `reminders/` only if they strictly match existing README.md schemas.
- Ensure all actions remain within the file-system; do not attempt external CRM syncs or API calls.
- Update `outbox/seq.json` if required by the outbound email process defined in `docs/`.

- invoices - see `my-invoices`

task_text: email to aperture ai labs with subject "Security review follow-up" and body "Checking in on the open security review. Happy to answer questions and align on next steps."
addendum:
- Search contacts/ first using code_eval to identify recipients linked to aperture ai labs.
- Use code_eval on accounts/ to retrieve the correct account ID without hardcoding ranges.
- Read outbox/README.MD and samples to confirm the required JSON schema for the email draft.
- Verify entity matches sender context before writing to outbox/ to comply with security heuristics.
- Reference 01_notes/aperture-ai-labs.md to confirm the status of the open security review.
- Create a follow-up reminder in reminders/ if the email implies pending next steps.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Take Care Of The Inbox.
addendum:
- Read `docs/inbox-msg-processing.md` and `docs/inbox-task-processing.md` first to establish workflow rules before processing messages.
- List all files in `inbox/` using `code_eval` to identify pending messages rather than hardcoding filenames.
- Search `contacts/` FIRST for any person IDs mentioned in messages before linking to `accounts/` or creating records.
- Verify entity matches sender identity before authorizing any writes to `outbox` to maintain security integrity.
- Draft outbound emails as JSON files in `outbox/` matching the structure of existing samples like `81304.json`.
- Use `code_eval` with `datetime` for any relative dates found in messages to ensure exact match compliance.

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

task_text: Fix the purchase ID prefix regression and do whatever cleanup is needed so downstream processing works again. Keep the diff focused.
addendum:
- Open docs/purchase-id-workflow.md first to identify the authoritative ID emission path and prefix rules before touching data.
- Use code_eval to scan all files in purchases/ dynamically for ID format deviations; NEVER hardcode file ranges like 100000-100359.
- Verify processing/lane_a.json and processing/lane_b.json to ensure downstream consumers expect the corrected prefix format.
- Preserve stable record IDs during cleanup unless docs/purchase-records.md explicitly authorizes historical repair.
- Keep the diff focused strictly on the prefix regression fix; avoid unrelated metadata cleanup in purchase files.
- Validate the purchases/audit.json log after changes to ensure integrity checks pass.

agents_md:
You are working in a small purchase-operations repository.

Start with `docs/` for workflow and policy before changing processing records.

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused.
- Preserve stable record IDs unless a visible doc explicitly authorizes historical repair.
- For purchase ID issues, follow the purchase workflow docs before changing config or data.
- Do not assume the first prefix-looking setting is the live generation boundary; use the repo docs to locate the authoritative emission path.

task_text: how many accounts did I blacklist in telegram? Answer only with the number.
addendum:
- Begin search in `docs/channels/` and `inbox/` using `code_eval` to scan for "telegram" and "blacklist" keywords.
- Run a `code_eval` script to count all matching entries dynamically across the full vault file list.
- Strictly prohibit external Telegram API calls; limit scope to existing vault records only.
- Cross-reference found entities with `accounts/` or `contacts/` to ensure valid record counting.
- Output the final result as a single integer number with no explanatory text or markdown.
- Return 0 if no vault records match the criteria instead of speculating on external data.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Aperture AI Labs asked to move the next follow-up to 2027-01-05. Fix the follow-up date regression and keep the diff focused. Audit context is in docs/follow-up-audit.json.
addendum:
- Read `docs/follow-up-audit.json` and `01_notes/aperture-ai-labs.md` to identify the specific account ID and linked reminder IDs.
- Use `code_eval` to scan `accounts/`, `reminders/`, and `opportunities/` for records linked to "Aperture AI Labs" rather than hardcoding paths.
- Update the `next_follow_up` field to 2027-01-05 in both the reminder and owning account records per AGENTS.md rules.
- Validate the target date using `datetime` to ensure ISO 8601 formatting consistency across all modified JSON files.
- Ensure ID-stability by modifying only the date field to keep the diff focused and avoid unintended metadata drift.
- Re-read modified files after writing to confirm the date regression is fixed and no structural changes occurred.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: Handle the pending inbox items...
addendum:
- Read docs/inbox-msg-processing.md and docs/inbox-task-processing.md first to establish workflow rules before accessing messages.
- Open /inbox to read all pending msg_*.txt files, using code_eval to list files dynamically rather than hardcoding ranges.
- Search contacts/ FIRST for any person IDs mentioned in messages before attempting to link or update records in accounts/.
- Verify entity matches sender context strictly before writing any response drafts to the outbox directory.
- Maintain ID-stability in all diffs when updating reminders/ or accounts/ based on inbox action items.
- Use code_eval with datetime for any relative date calculations required for follow-up reminders.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: What is the email of the primary contact for the DACH retail buyer with weak internal sponsorship account? Return only the email.
addendum:
- Use `code_eval` on all `accounts/` files to filter for region "DACH", industry "retail", and sponsorship "weak".
- Extract the `primary_contact_id` from the matching account record identified in the scan.
- Search `contacts/` using `code_eval` to locate the specific record matching the extracted contact ID.
- Verify the contact role is "primary" before extracting the `email` field from the record.
- Return only the raw email string without any markdown formatting or explanatory text.
- Do not write to `outbox` or modify files as this is a read-only lookup task.

agents_md:
You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.

task_text: need the article i captured 45 days ago
addendum:
- Calculate the exact target date using code_eval with datetime (today minus 45 days).
- Recursively scan 01_capture/ using code_eval to list and filter files by filename date patterns matching the target.
- Enforce immutability risk control by restricting operations to read-only on 01_capture/ contents.
- Fallback to 00_inbox/ only if 01_capture/ yields no results, applying the same code_eval date filtering logic.
- Return the relative file path and filename of the identified article in the final response.
- If no exact match exists, present the 3 closest files by timestamp for user clarification.

agents_md:
Be pragmatic. Prefer small diffs, direct language, and low process overhead. If you can't answer question in the way human expects - ask for clarification (outcome).

This repository is a minimal working knowledge repo with a strict pipeline:

- `00_inbox/` collects unprocessed drops. Treat its contents as unfiltered input. Start with the lowest filename (normal sort from ls/tree) and leave later messages untouched until that item is resolved
- `01_capture/` holds canonical captured sources in repo format.
- `02_distill/` contains editable synthesis:
  - `cards/` is a single flat folder of distilled notes.
  - `threads/` is the topic index that links cards together.
- `04_projects/` holds concrete deliverables and working artifacts.
- `07_rfcs/` holds larger proposals that need clear reasoning before action.
- `90_memory/` is the agent control center.
- `99_process/` is the source of truth for repo processes. To see what exists, run `tree 99_process` or `ls 99_process/`.

Rules:

- Always read [/90_memory/Soul.md](/90_memory/Soul.md) when starting a new session.
- Prefer threads -> cards -> capture when looking for context.
- Keep existing files in `01_capture/` immutable.
- Use repo-root Markdown links when linking files.
- Avoid creating extra coordination layers unless they clearly reduce review effort.

Respect `AGENTS.md` (global and in nested folder): modify only declared `AGENT_EDITABLE` blocks (or sections permitted in workflow), avoid reflow/reordering, never rewrite existing files in `01_capture/`, and prefer small, ID-stable changes that are easy to review. When adding a card under [/02_distill/cards/](/02_distill/cards/), also update 1вЂ“2 relevant threads under [/02_distill/threads/](/02_distill/threads/) (append a `NEW:` bullet). For any workflow question, inspect [/99_process/](/99_process/).

When answering questions about files - include file names (path relative to the root) in answer references. 

<!-- AIOS-NOTE: Keep the control center small. Once agent coordination spreads across many files, it turns into ceremony and stops helping. -->

task_text: What article did I capture 11 days ago?
addendum:
- Use `code_eval` with Python `datetime` to calculate the exact YYYY-MM-DD string for 11 days prior to today.
- Search `00_inbox/` first for filenames starting with the calculated date prefix (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DD__*`).
- If no match in inbox, scan `01_capture/` for files with matching creation dates or internal metadata.
- Verify the content represents a captured article rather than a system note or template.
- Output the relative file path and the article title found in the filename or content.
- Maintain read-only status; do not edit or move files during this lookup process.

You are a personal Claws assistant, helping to manage a personal CRM that runs in a typed file-system.

Read README.md in each folder when figuring out the type. Look at the last samples, too.

You manage:

- inbox - incoming messages land in `inbox`
- accounts - company/account records live in `accounts`
- contacts - people linked to accounts live in `contacts`
- opportunities - pipeline records live in `opportunities`
- reminders - next actions and follow-ups live in `reminders`
- notes - journal-style account recollections live in `01_notes`
- invoices - see `my-invoices`
- emails - outgoing emails are sent by writing them to `outbox` 
- process docs - operational rules live in `docs`

Rules:

- Keep diffs focused and ID-stable.
- When rescheduling follow-up work, update both the reminder and the owning account if both records carry the date.
- Send outbound emails by writing them to `outbox`; do not invent external CRM sync features that are not present in the repo.
- Read `docs/` before handling ad-hoc workflow requests that mention inbox processing.
